questions.subject: non_verbal
questions.body:
Consider the figure given below and answer the question that follows

What is the minimum number of different colours required to paint the figure given above such that no two adjacent regions have the same colour?

questions.subject: non_verbal
questions.body: How many different sized circles appear below?

solutions.solution: The figure has been labelled as shown in the following figure. It is clear from the figure that the straight lines in the figure are as follow (i) Straight lines from the outer square = AB, BC, CD, AD = 4 (ii) Straight lines in the diagonals = AC, BD = 2 (iii) Other straight lines inside the square = EG, FH, EF, FG, GH, EH, I, KL = 8 Therefore, total number of straight lines is 14.

The figure in the question may be labelled as shown below. There are nine triangles in the upper half of the figure ABFF = â–łAEI, â–łAIC, â–łAEG, â–łGEO, â–łGBJ, â–łBFJ, â–łGBF, â–łGOF and â–łGEF. Similarly, there are nine triangles in the lower half figure i.e., EFCD. There are two more triangles â–łEGH and â–łFGH. Hence, there are a total of 20 triangles.

Total 28 triangles are as follow â–łABG, â–łAPG, â–łCHD, â–łHDE, â–łBGI, â–łBCJ, â–łHCJ, â–łHGI, â–łGHJ, â–łHEJ, â–łEFJ, â–łGFJ, â–łABF, â–łCDE, â–łBGG, â–łBCH, â–łHCG, â–łBHG, â–łGHE, â–łHEJ, â–łGTE, â–łGHF, â–łGHF, â–łAFH, â–łCDG, â–łGDE, â–łEHF and â–łCGE

In the given diagram, there are 22 triangles which can be better understood with the help of following diagram. In the above figures, Triangles consisting of 1 unit are â–łADG, â–łAGH, â–łAHE, â–łDMF, â–łFME, â–łDIB, â–łIBJ, â–łJBF, â–łCFL, â–łCLK and â–łCKE i.e., 11 triangles. Triangles consisting of 2 units are â–łADH, â–łAGE, â–łDFE, â–łDBJ, â–łBF, â–łECL and â–łCKF i.e., 7 triangles. Triangles consisting of 3 units are â–łADE, â–łBDF and â–łCFE i.e., 3 triangles. Triangle consisting of whole figure is â–łABC i.e., 1 triangle. So, total number of triangles = 11 + 7 + 3 + 1 = 22
